rationale: The cited paper appears directly relevant as a potential mechanism source: it shows that visual arrangement (horizontal vs. vertical display) changes processing fluency and downstream choice, while the citing paper centers on interface structure effects and explicitly tests interface fluency as the mediator. Both notes emphasize layout/interface form influencing consumer processing and outcomes, suggesting more than a generic background mention. Even though the raw evidence is only a DOI-level bibliography match, this conceptual overlap is strong enough to retain for full relation judgment.
